title: The Birds, The Peacock And His Feathers
narrative_elements: birds request feathers in exchange for praise
peacock gives away all feathers
peacock dies unable to care for chicks
chicks abandoned

title: The Jackdaw And The Peacocks
narrative_elements: jackdaw adorns himself with peacock feathers
peacocks attack and expose jackdaw
jackdaw rejected by own kind
jackdaw warned about accepting one's nature

title: Hermes And Tiresias
narrative_elements: hermes steals cattle to test tiresias
hermes accompanies tiresias in disguise
tiresias interprets bird omens
tiresias reveals he knows the thief

title: Juno And The Peacock
narrative_elements: peacock complains about his voice to juno
juno lists peacock's gifts of beauty
peacock laments that beauty is silent
juno explains each creature has its assigned gift

title: The Crow, The Eagle And The Feathers
narrative_elements: crow borrows feathers from other birds
crow mocks other birds in borrowed plumage
other birds complain to eagle
eagle orders feathers returned, humiliating crow

title: The Dove And The Crow
narrative_elements: dove boasts about having many children
crow rebukes dove by pointing out that more children means more slaves

title: The Travellers And The Crow
narrative_elements: crow envies raven's prophetic power
crow squawks at passing travellers hoping to be heeded
travellers dismiss crow as meaningless

title: The Eagle And The Crow
narrative_elements: eagle carries tortoise but cannot open shell
crow offers advice in exchange for share of food
crow advises eagle to drop tortoise on rocks
eagle shares feast with crow

title: The Eagle And The Crow
narrative_elements: eagle struggles to open tortoise shell
crow offers to help in exchange for share
crow instructs eagle to drop shell from height
crow steals tortoise when it falls

title: The Cyclops And His Treasure
narrative_elements: man loses wealth and plans suicide
man discovers cyclops' hidden gold in a pit
man abandons sword and returns home with gold
cyclops finds only sword and kills himself

title: The Woman And Her Two Daughters
narrative_elements: mother visits daughter married to gardener who wants rain
mother visits daughter married to potter who wants sunshine
mother realizes she cannot pray for both contradictory wishes

title: The Poor Man And Death
narrative_elements: poor man collapses under wood burden and calls for death
death appears immediately
man asks death only to help lift the bundle

title: The Lion, The Hare And The Deer
narrative_elements: lion finds sleeping hare but spots a deer
lion abandons hare to chase deer
lion fails to catch deer
lion returns to find hare has escaped

title: The Deer And Her Friends
narrative_elements: stag falls ill and gathers food
animals visit under pretense of concern
animals eat stag's food stores
stag starves to death

title: The Deer And The Lion
narrative_elements: deer flees hunters and hides in lion's cave
lion attacks the deer
deer laments fleeing into greater danger

title: The Deer And The Vine
narrative_elements: deer hides from hunters under a grapevine
hunters pass by
deer eats the vine leaves that sheltered her
hunter spots deer and kills her

title: The Hunters And The Deer With One Eye
narrative_elements: one-eyed deer watches land for hunters
deer turns blind eye toward sea thinking it safe
hunters arrive by sea and shoot the deer
deer laments fearing the wrong danger

title: The Man, The Pig And The Miracle
narrative_elements: man unable to repay debt brings pig to sell
man makes miraculous claim about pig's litters
creditor joins in fabricating claims about pig
story exposes comic complicity in deception

title: Diogenes And The Ferryman
narrative_elements: ferryman carries diogenes across flooded river
diogenes laments being unable to repay
ferryman helps another traveller
diogenes decides the ferryman acts from habit not virtue

title: The Patient And His Symptoms
narrative_elements: doctor reassures patient every symptom is positive
patient's condition worsens through fever and chills
patient says he is dying of positive symptoms

title: The Old Woman And Her Doctor
narrative_elements: woman hires doctor to cure eye ailment
doctor smears ointment blinding her and steals her possessions
woman is cured but all property is gone
woman protests she cannot see her belongings

title: The Doctor At The Funeral
narrative_elements: doctor attends relative's funeral
doctor says patient could have survived with proper treatment
bystander rebukes doctor for useless after-the-fact advice

title: The Donkey, The Dog And The Letter
narrative_elements: donkey and dog find a sealed letter
donkey reads letter aloud about hay and barley
dog grows impatient and asks for meat content
dog dismisses letter as useless

title: The Dog And The Lion
narrative_elements: dog chases lion confidently
lion turns and roars
dog flees in panic
fox mocks dog for chasing what he cannot face

title: The Fox, The Lamb And The Dog
narrative_elements: fox enters sheep flock and seizes a lamb under pretense of affection
dog questions fox's behavior
dog threatens fox with violence if she doesn't release the lamb

title: The Wolves And The Hides
narrative_elements: wolves see cowhides in flooded river and want them
wolves plan to drink the river dry
man warns this will kill them

title: The Shepherd, The Dog And The Sick Sheep
narrative_elements: dog is fed on dead sheep by shepherd
dog stands near sick lamb seeming sorrowful
shepherd rebukes dog for false sympathy
shepherd suspects dog wants the lamb to die

title: The Donkey And The Pet Dog
narrative_elements: donkey envies pet dog's pampered treatment
donkey attempts to imitate dog's fawning behavior
donkey leaps on master and causes chaos
servants beat donkey back to stable

title: The Farmer And His Dogs
narrative_elements: farmer trapped by winter storm eats his sheep then goats
farmer slaughters oxen that labour for him
dogs witness this and fear for themselves
dogs decide to flee before the farmer can turn on them

title: The Wolves And The Dogs
narrative_elements: wolves propose brotherhood to dogs citing their shared nature
wolves promise freedom from servitude
dogs are persuaded and abandon their flock
wolves kill dogs then massacre the flock

title: The Mother Dog And Her Puppies
narrative_elements: mother dog borrows another dog's kennel to give birth
owner asks for kennel back after birth
mother dog pleads for more time until puppies grow strong
mother dog and grown pups threaten owner and refuse to leave

title: The Dog, The Meat And The Reflection
narrative_elements: dog steals meat and crosses a river
dog sees reflection and thinks it is larger piece of meat
dog drops real meat to snatch at reflection
raven grabs the dropped meat and dog is left with nothing

title: The Fox, The Rooster And The Dog
narrative_elements: rooster and dog travel together and rest in a tree
fox tries to lure rooster down with flattery
rooster tricks fox into announcing herself to the watchman
dog leaps up and kills fox

title: The Dog And The Lamb Among The Goats
narrative_elements: dog finds lamb bleating among goats
dog points to sheep flock and says that is his mother
lamb argues the nursing mother who feeds him matters more than birth mother
lamb makes philosophical case for nurturing over biological kinship

title: The Sheep, The Dog And The Wolf
narrative_elements: dog falsely accuses sheep of owing him bread
wolf testifies falsely that sheep owes ten loaves
sheep is unjustly convicted and forced to pay
wolf later found dead in ditch and sheep calls it divine justice

title: The Sheep, The Dog And The Witnesses
narrative_elements: dog falsely claims sheep borrowed bread
wolf and kite swear false testimony against sheep
court convicts sheep based on three corrupt witnesses
sheep must shear herself to pay back debt she never incurred

title: The Dog And The Shellfish
narrative_elements: dog habitually swallows eggs whole
dog encounters shellfish and mistakes it for an egg
dog swallows shellfish and suffers stomach pain
dog laments his assumption that all round things are eggs

title: The Sow, The Dog And Their Litters
narrative_elements: dog boasts she has easiest delivery of all four-footed animals
sow concedes the point but retorts that dog's puppies are born blind

title: The Dog And The Treasure
narrative_elements: dog digs up treasure while scavenging bones
spirits of the dead punish dog with greed for wealth
dog guards treasure and forgets to eat
vulture mocks dog for abandoning his true nature in pursuit of riches

title: The Wolf And The Sleeping Dog
narrative_elements: wolf finds thin dog sleeping at barn and wants to eat him
dog begs wolf to wait until wedding fattens him up
wolf returns and finds dog sleeping on roof
dog warns wolf never to trust his promise again

title: The Wolf And The Dog In Pursuit
narrative_elements: dog chases wolf and capers with pride at his speed
dog imagines wolf is fleeing from his superiority
wolf turns and corrects dog that it is the master he fears
wolf deflates dog's illusion of power

title: The Dog And The Gardener
narrative_elements: dog falls into gardener's well
gardener climbs down to rescue the dog
dog bites gardener thinking he is being pushed deeper
gardener laments his effort to save the ungrateful dog

title: The Dog And The Hare
narrative_elements: dog chases and catches hare
dog alternately bites hare and licks blood from wound
hare mistakes biting and licking for affection
hare demands dog choose between friend and enemy

title: The Dog-Catcher And The Dog
narrative_elements: dog-catcher throws food to a passing dog
dog rejects the kindness as suspicious
dog warns the man to keep away
dog recognizes that unsolicited goodwill is a warning sign

title: The Hunting Dog And The Watch Dog
narrative_elements: master has hunting dog and watch dog
hunting dog catches prey while watch dog guards home
hunting dog resents watch dog eating from his labour
watch dog says blame the master who taught him only to eat

title: The Wolves, The Sheep And The Dogs
narrative_elements: wolves lurk near sheep flock and are kept away by dogs
wolves propose peace and blame dogs for hostility
sheep foolishly dismiss dogs
wolves devour the unguarded flock

title: The Tuna Fish And The Dolphin
narrative_elements: dolphin chases tuna fish through the water
tuna fish leaps onto an island to escape
dolphin runs aground alongside tuna fish
tuna fish accepts death knowing his pursuer shares his fate

title: The Wolf And The Raven
narrative_elements: raven perches on donkey's back and pecks at wound
donkey brays in pain while driver laughs
wolf observes the double standard
wolf laments wolves are driven away while ravens tormenting donkeys are smiled at

title: The Fox, The Donkey And The Lion
narrative_elements: fox and donkey hunt together and encounter a lion
fox offers to betray donkey in exchange for her own life
fox leads donkey into a trap
lion seizes fox first after donkey is trapped

title: The Fox, The Donkey And The Lion Skin
narrative_elements: donkey puts on lion skin and frightens all animals
donkey approaches fox hoping to frighten her too
fox had already heard the donkey's bray
fox tells donkey she would have been frightened if not for his voice

title: The Goat And The Donkey
narrative_elements: goat envies donkey's larger food portion and tricks him into feigning a seizure
donkey follows goat's advice and falls into a ditch
doctor says goat's lungs will cure the donkey
goat is slaughtered and becomes victim of her own scheme

title: The Donkey, The Horse And The War
narrative_elements: horse enjoys special privileges while donkey labours under heavy burdens
owner rides horse into battle
horse is wounded repeatedly in combat
donkey congratulates himself on his humble life of labour upon seeing horse degraded

title: The Donkey And The Horse
narrative_elements: proud horse threatens to crush donkey with his hooves
donkey groans and calls on gods to witness his suffering
horse is broken down by racing and sent to work on farm
donkey mocks horse for the same wretchedness he formerly scorned

title: The Donkey, The Horse And The Barley
narrative_elements: donkey asks horse for a nibble of barley
horse refuses but promises a whole sack that evening
donkey questions why he should trust a big future promise from someone who won't give a small favour now

title: The Donkey And The Lion Go Hunting
narrative_elements: lion hides donkey in bushes to frighten prey with his braying
donkey brays loudly causing animals to flee into lion's ambush
lion praises donkey's efforts with a deflating compliment
lion admits he too would have fled if he didn't know it was a donkey

title: The Donkey And The Lyre
narrative_elements: donkey finds a lyre in a field
donkey strikes the strings with his hoof and they resound
donkey laments the instrument is wasted on him as he knows nothing of music
donkey wishes someone more capable had found it

title: The Donkey And The Mule
narrative_elements: donkey resents mule getting double food for equal load
driver repeatedly transfers donkey's load to mule as donkey tires
driver transfers entire load to mule
mule demonstrates to donkey that the extra food was earned by the extra work

title: The Donkey And His Masters
narrative_elements: donkey prays to zeus to be taken from overworked gardener
zeus sends donkey to potter who loads him even more heavily
donkey prays again and is sent to tanner
donkey laments that his final master will not even give him a proper burial

title: The Donkey, The Onager And The Lion
narrative_elements: onager mocks donkey for being enslaved and overworked
onager boasts of his freedom on the hillsides
lion appears and ignores guarded donkey
lion attacks and devours the unprotected onager

title: The Old Man, The Donkey And The Pack Saddles
narrative_elements: enemy approaches and old man urges donkey to flee
donkey asks whether victor will make him carry two saddles
old man says no
donkey concludes it makes no difference who the master is

title: The Man And The New Donkey
narrative_elements: man selects donkey on trial and leaves him with his herd
new donkey immediately chooses laziest donkey as companion
man returns donkey without further testing
man concludes the donkey reveals itself by the company it chooses

title: The Pig, The Donkey And The Barley
narrative_elements: man sacrifices pig to fulfil vow to hercules
man offers leftover barley to donkey
donkey refuses to eat food tainted by the pig's slaughter
donkey makes wry comment about appetite and death

title: Zeus And The Donkeys
narrative_elements: donkeys petition zeus to be freed from toil
zeus says suffering ends when they piss a river
donkeys take the impossible condition literally
donkeys forever stop to urinate where another donkey has gone

title: The Lion, The Rooster And The Donkey
narrative_elements: lion approaches to attack donkey but is scared by rooster's crow
donkey wrongly believes he frightened the lion
donkey pursues the fleeing lion away from the farm
lion turns and devours the donkey once safe from the rooster

title: The Driver And The Donkey On The Cliff
narrative_elements: donkey strays from road toward cliff
driver tries to pull donkey back by its tail
donkey resists all pulling and keeps moving forward
driver gives up and sarcastically lets donkey win its fatal contest

title: The Jackdaw And The Doves
narrative_elements: jackdaw dyes himself white to infiltrate dovecote
doves accept him while he stays silent
jackdaw forgets himself and squawks revealing his identity
doves expel him and his own kind no longer recognize him leaving him starving

title: The Fox And The Dragon
narrative_elements: fox digs her den and accidentally reaches dragon's cave
dragon guards treasure assigned by jupiter
fox asks what reward dragon receives for lifelong guarding in the dark
dragon admits he has no reward and fox concludes he was born under an unlucky star

title: The Piece Of Driftwood
narrative_elements: men spot distant object at sea and assume it is a great ship
they wait expecting it to land nearby
object gradually reveals itself as smaller and smaller
it turns out to be a piece of driftwood and men lament their foolish expectations

title: The Woman And Her Drunken Husband
narrative_elements: wife devises plan to cure husband's drinking by leaving him in cemetery
wife knocks pretending to bring food to the dead
drunk husband asks only for drink not food
wife despairs that her scheme has made him worse not better

title: The Peacock Elected King Of The Birds
narrative_elements: birds assemble to choose a king
peacock claims kingship based on beauty
birds are satisfied with peacock's claim
raven challenges whether peacock can protect them from eagle's attacks

title: The Hares And The Foxes
narrative_elements: hares are at war with eagle and seek fox allies
foxes decline citing knowledge of hares' cowardly nature
foxes also note the fearsome power of the enemy

title: The Two Roosters And The Eagle
narrative_elements: two roosters fight for dominance
loser hides in corner in shame
winner flies to rooftop crowing in triumph
eagle swoops down and carries off the boasting victor

title: The Eagle And The Fox
narrative_elements: eagle and fox become friends and nest near each other
eagle steals fox's cubs while fox is away to feed her eaglets
fox is powerless to avenge her cubs directly
eagle steals burning cinder from altar and her nest catches fire killing her eaglets which fox devours

title: The Hare And The Sparrow
narrative_elements: eagle seizes hare and hare weeps
sparrow mocks hare's famed swiftness while he suffers
hawk suddenly kills sparrow mid-taunt
dying hare finds comfort that sparrow's fate mirrors his own

title: The Eagle And The Kite
narrative_elements: eagle seeks a worthy mate
kite boasts he can catch an ostrich to win eagle's hand
eagle accepts and marries kite
kite can only offer a rotten mouse and admits he lied to secure the match

title: The Earthworm And The Snake
narrative_elements: earthworm envies snake's length
earthworm lies beside snake and tries to stretch himself
earthworm stretches until he splits into pieces

title: The Two Enemies
narrative_elements: enemies board same ship and take opposite ends
storm wrecks the ship
man at stern asks which end will sink first
man at stern accepts his own death gladly knowing enemy dies first

title: The Farmer, The Wolf And The Plow
narrative_elements: farmer leaves oxen to drink and departs from plow
wolf finds plow and licks at yoke straps tasting oxen
wolf's neck becomes trapped in the yoke while licking
wolf starts dragging the plow and farmer sarcastically wishes wolf would abandon theft for farming

title: The Race Horse In The Mill
narrative_elements: prized race horse grows old and is sold to grind at a mill
horse is harnessed to millstone and works all day
horse laments trading the glory of the racetrack for endless circling in a mill

title: The Eunuch And His Accuser
narrative_elements: accuser insults eunuch in court for his physical condition
eunuch admits his situation makes legal testimony difficult
eunuch rebukes accuser for condemning what is merely a matter of fate

title: The Kite And The Partridges
narrative_elements: kite admires his talons and decides to hunt like hawk and falcon
kite attacks partridges seizing one in beak and one in each talon
kite cannot hold all four prey at once and loses them all
kite never tries to catch wild birds again

title: The Stag Among The Cattle
narrative_elements: stag flees hunters and hides in an ox stall
oxen warn stag he has sealed his doom by hiding among humans
farm workers and bailiff pass through without noticing stag
master inspects manger carefully and spots the stag's horns ordering him killed

title: The Farmer And The Fox
narrative_elements: farmer envies neighbour's crops and plans to destroy them
farmer ties a burning brand to fox's tail and releases her
fox runs the wrong way and sets fire to the farmer's own crops
poetic justice ruins the schemer not his victim

title: The Lion And The Farmer
narrative_elements: lion enters farmer's yard and farmer locks the gate to trap him
lion devours sheep and then cattle
farmer opens gate in fear to let lion escape
farmer's wife rebukes him for trapping himself with a creature one should flee at a distance

title: The Farmer, The Wasps And The Partridges
narrative_elements: wasps and partridges beg farmer for water offering services in return
partridges promise to tend his vines
wasps promise to guard against thieves
farmer says his oxen serve him without bargaining and gives water to them instead

title: The Farmer, The Cattle And The Manure
narrative_elements: cattle haul manure from stables and complain it is vile work
cattle point out they provide all the farmer's grain year round
farmer asks whether they themselves produced the manure
farmer concludes those who make the mess must clean it up

title: The Snake And The Farmer
narrative_elements: snake lives in farmer's house and brings him good fortune
farmer grows rich but then attacks snake with axe
farmer loses wealth and begs snake's forgiveness
snake says wound cannot be forgotten and true reconciliation is impossible while the scar remains

title: The Snake And The Farmer
narrative_elements: snake bites farmer's son who dies instantly
father chases snake with axe but only cuts off tip of its tail
farmer brings offerings and tries to make peace with snake
snake refuses friendship saying mutual wounds cannot be forgotten

title: The Wolves And The Harvesters
narrative_elements: wolves lament being universally hated even when innocent
wolves decide to help harvesters gather sheaves to prove goodwill
harvesters chase wolves away screaming despite their helpful behaviour
wolves conclude that whether they help or harm people hatred will follow them regardless
